note_sources.content:
### EP-545
- Quick literature check:
  - Page notes small-$m$ failures of the "as complete as possible" maximizer heuristic.
- Finite compute signal:
  - Exact diagonal checks recover:
    - $R(P_3)=3$
    - $R(2K_2)=5$
    - $R(K_3)=6$
- Interpretation:
  - Explicitly confirms the counterexample pattern at $m=2$: $R(2K_2)>R(P_3)$.

script_sources.heading: small-m exact counterexample check for "as complete as possible" maximizer.

data.description: Exact tiny-m diagonal Ramsey checks showing small-edge counterexample behavior.
data.explicit_m2_counterexample: R(2K2)=5 > R(P3)=3 where P3 is the as-complete-as-possible graph for m=2.
